sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Epilepsies in children, young people and adults
sources.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or established epilepsy.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, specialist, emergency, and ongoing epilepsy care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for epilepsy assessment, monitoring, information, emergency planning, and risk review; international only and local driving, referral, and prescribing rules require UAE review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.3.7 — seizure assessment, EEG, and imaging
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure-event and witness history, physical examination, ECG or metabolic context, EEG review, MRI review, recurrence after remission, and specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1.1–2.1.11 — information, safety, and daily impact
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports trigger, adherence, side-effect, safety, driving, employment or education, reproductive-health, and care-plan discussions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.1.1–4.5.11 — treatment, monitoring, and review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure-control and adverse-effect review, clinician-led medication and interaction review, antiseizure medication levels when indicated, care review, and pregnancy-related specialist review without mapping a dose.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 7.1.1–7.3.1 — status, cluster, and prolonged seizure plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation of seizure clusters or convulsive emergencies and a clinician-agreed individual emergency management plan without mapping emergency medication or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 10.1.1–10.2.2 — epilepsy-related death and SUDEP risk
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports seizure frequency, adherence, alcohol or drug use, uncontrolled seizures, abnormal neurological findings, and clinician-documented risk and safety discussion.

archive.title: Epilepsies in children, young people and adults
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or established epilepsy.
archive.setting: Primary, specialist, emergency, and ongoing epilepsy care.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral
sources.population: Adults aged over 16 years with suspected neurological conditions.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial assessment and referral from primary or community care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected adult dizziness, vertigo, limb weakness, sensory symptoms, and tremor referral features; not applicable to children and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — dizziness and vertigo in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports focal neurological and hearing red flags, acute vestibular syndrome, HINTS by trained clinicians, neuroimaging referral, and Hallpike assessment for positional vertigo.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.7.10 and 1.10.1–1.10.6 — limb weakness and sensory symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden, progressive, fluctuating, unilateral, symmetrical, sensory-associated, and back-pain-associated weakness, urgent referral, and clinician-led imaging where specified.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.13 — sensory symptoms including tingling or numbness in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.15.1–1.15.4 — tremor in adults
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports rest or asymmetric tremor, stiffness, slowness, balance or gait difficulty, symmetrical postural tremor, medicine, thyroid and alcohol review, functional impact, head tremor, and clinician-led referral.
